data.title: Goldstone Capital Director’s Passing Triggers Temporary Non-Compliance with HK Listing Rules
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest update is out from Youth Champ Financial Group Holdings Limited ( (HK:1160) ).

Goldstone Capital Group Limited has announced the passing of independent non-executive director Mr. Lu Zhicheng, who also served as chairman of the Remuneration Committee and a member of both the Audit and Nomination Committees. His death reduces the number of independent non-executive directors and committee members, causing the company to fall temporarily out of compliance with Hong Kong listing requirements regarding board independence, audit committee size and remuneration committee leadership. The board plans to appoint a new independent non-executive director and committee member, including a new Remuneration Committee chair, within three months of 24 January 2026 to restore full compliance and will issue a further announcement once the appointment is made.

More about Youth Champ Financial Group Holdings Limited

Goldstone Capital Group Limited is a Bermuda-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 1160. Its board currently consists of one executive director, three non-executive directors and two independent non-executive directors.

Average Trading Volume: 300,000

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$145.9M

data.title: Lianlian DigiTech Formalizes Audit Committee Mandate to Bolster Governance
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest update is out from Lianlian DigiTech Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2598) ).

Lianlian DigiTech Co., Ltd. has formalized the terms of reference for its Board-level Audit Committee to enhance corporate governance and strengthen oversight of its financial reporting and control environment. The committee, composed entirely of non-executive directors with a majority of independent non-executive directors and at least one member possessing accounting or financial expertise, will be responsible for supervising internal and external audits, reviewing financial reports and disclosures, assessing internal control effectiveness, and proposing the appointment or replacement of external auditors, thereby centralizing supervisory functions previously associated with a supervisory committee and aligning the company more closely with Hong Kong Listing Rules and Chinese regulatory requirements.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2598) stock is a Hold with a HK$7.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Lianlian DigiTech Co., Ltd. Class H stock, see the HK:2598 Stock Forecast page.

Lianlian DigiTech Co., Ltd. is a China-based financial technology company that operates in the digital payments and related services sector, focusing on providing technology-driven transaction, settlement and value-added financial services to enterprises and consumers, with its shares listed in Hong Kong as Class H shares.

Lianlian DigiTech Co., Ltd. reported that its extraordinary general meeting held in Hangzhou on 26 January 2026 was duly convened in compliance with PRC laws, Hong Kong listing rules and the company’s articles, with all directors attending and voting conducted by poll under independent scrutiny. data.description:

Lianlian DigiTech Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2598) ) has issued an announcement.

Lianlian DigiTech Co., Ltd. has formally set out detailed terms of reference for its Board Nomination Committee, a specialised governance body tasked with proposing candidates for directors, including independent non-executive directors, and senior management. The document specifies the committee’s composition, requiring three directors with a majority being independent non-executive directors, and sets out how members and the chairperson are nominated, elected, and replaced, as well as their required qualifications and tenure rules. By codifying strict eligibility criteria, clear appointment procedures, and safeguards for continuity of duties when members resign or become disqualified, the company is strengthening its board-level governance framework, enhancing transparency in leadership selection, and aligning its corporate practices more closely with PRC securities regulations and Hong Kong listing requirements, which is likely to be reassuring for investors and other stakeholders focused on oversight and compliance.

Qidian International Co., Ltd. ( (HK:1280) ) has issued an announcement.

China Qidian Guofeng Holdings’ subsidiary Shanghai Huiliu Network Technology has signed a one-year, non-exclusive leading channel distributor agreement with MetaX Integrated Circuits to distribute and promote MetaX’s full series of GPU products across Mainland China, targeting major internet firms, intelligent computing centres, research institutions and enterprises with high AI computing power needs. data.title: China Galaxy Securities Raises RMB6 Billion in First Tranche of 2026 Corporate Bonds
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An update from China Galaxy Securities Co ( (HK:6881) ) is now available.

China Galaxy Securities Co., Ltd. has completed the first tranche of its 2026 public corporate bond issuance to professional investors, raising RMB6 billion under a broader debt financing authorization that allows issuances up to 350% of its latest audited net assets. The offering comprises two-year bonds of RMB1.5 billion with a 1.79% coupon and three-year bonds of RMB4.5 billion with a 1.85% coupon, with proceeds earmarked to supplement working capital, thereby strengthening liquidity and funding flexibility. Several lead underwriters and their related parties, including China CITIC Bank, CSC Financial, China Resources SZITIC Trust, and Everbright Securities, subscribed to portions of the bonds, while the company’s directors, senior management, and major shareholders did not participate, underscoring reliance on institutional market demand and reinforcing its position in China’s onshore bond market.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:6881) stock is a Buy with a HK$13.99 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Galaxy Securities Co stock, see the HK:6881 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Galaxy Securities Co

China Galaxy Securities Co., Ltd. is a PRC-incorporated securities firm listed in Hong Kong, operating in the financial services industry. The company focuses on securities brokerage, investment banking, and related capital market services for professional and institutional investors in China and overseas markets.

An update from JiaHua Stores Holdings Limited ( (HK:0602) ) is now available.

Jiahua Stores Holdings Limited has entered into a long-term lease agreement, together with related individual lease agreements, for a portfolio of properties to be used for its shopping mall, retail shops and other commercial operations, with the leases requiring recognition of right-of-use assets valued at approximately RMB177.8 million on its balance sheet. The transaction qualifies as a very substantial acquisition and a connected transaction under Hong Kong listing rules because part of the leased premises is owned by a company ultimately controlled by the founding Zhuang family, triggering reporting, circular and independent shareholder approval requirements; an Independent Board Committee and independent financial adviser have been appointed, conflicted directors have abstained from board voting, related parties will abstain from voting at the forthcoming extraordinary general meeting, and a detailed circular is to be issued to shareholders by 27 February 2026.

data.title: Renco Holdings Given New Hurdles for Share Trading Resumption Amid Liquidation
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Renco Holdings Group Ltd. ( (HK:2323) ) has provided an announcement.

Renco Holdings Group Limited, a Bermuda-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong and now in compulsory liquidation, has seen its directors’ powers cease following a 2025 winding-up order, leaving joint and several liquidators in control of its operations and assets. The company has received additional resumption guidance from the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, requiring it to re-comply with various corporate governance and board composition rules, secure the withdrawal or dismissal of the winding-up order and discharge of liquidators, and publish all outstanding financial results while addressing any audit issues before trading in its shares can resume; until these conditions are met to the exchange’s satisfaction, trading in Renco’s shares will remain suspended, prolonging uncertainty for shareholders and creditors.

More about Renco Holdings Group Ltd.

Renco Holdings Group Limited is a Bermuda-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, currently in compulsory liquidation. Its board powers have been terminated following a winding-up order issued by the Hong Kong High Court in August 2025, with the company’s affairs, business and assets now managed by joint and several liquidators acting as agents of the company without personal liability.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$50.31M

data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Sunshine Lake Pharma Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:6887) ) has issued an update.

Sunshine Lake Pharma has entered into a strategic cooperation framework with Shenzhen XtalPi Technology to establish an AI-driven drug R&D platform through a planned joint venture, reflecting its push to deepen digital and automated capabilities in drug innovation. The partners intend to set up a joint laboratory to co-develop innovative autoimmune drug pipelines, build and commercialize large AI models including a predictive PBPK model and the company’s existing SynAI and PharmAI platforms, and create a Model-as-a-Service business based on an AI supercomputing and data-sharing ecosystem, a move the board views as creating significant synergy and potential long-term benefits for the company and its shareholders, though no binding agreements have yet been signed and implementation remains uncertain.

More about Sunshine Lake Pharma Co., Ltd. Class H

Sunshine Lake Pharma Co., Ltd. is a research-driven international pharmaceutical group founded in 2003, specializing in pediatric medicine, infectious diseases, chronic diseases and oncology. The company has 150 approved drugs and more than 100 under development, including nearly 50 first-in-class innovative drugs, and has consistently ranked among the top Chinese chemical pharma companies by R&D strength and patent output, supported by its proprietary HEC intelligent drug discovery platforms such as the HEC-SynAI Drug Synthesis Model and HEC-PharmAI Drug Preparation Model.

An announcement from MicroPort CardioFlow Medtech Corp. ( (HK:2160) ) is now available.

MicroPort CardioFlow Medtech Corp. has proposed a share consolidation that will combine every five existing shares into one consolidated share, raising the par value per share while keeping the company’s total authorised share capital unchanged at US$50,000. Subject to shareholder approval at an extraordinary general meeting and the Hong Kong Stock Exchange’s listing approval, the move would reduce the number of issued shares from about 6.37 billion to roughly 1.27 billion without affecting the board lot size, a technical capital restructuring that could influence trading dynamics and share price optics but does not alter underlying ownership proportions.

data.title: Li Ning Invests RMB1.9 Billion in Low-Risk Structured Deposits
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Li Ning Company ( (HK:2331) ) just unveiled an update.

Li Ning Company Limited has deployed a total of RMB1.9 billion of surplus cash into low-risk structured deposit products through several wholly owned subsidiaries between 18 and 26 January 2026. The funds were placed with major Chinese banks, including China Guangfa Bank, Bank of China and China Merchants Bank, in multiple tranches ranging from RMB200 million to RMB700 million, with product tenors of about five to six months and expected annual returns between 0.60% and 3.00%. As these wealth management subscriptions occurred within a 12‑month period, they are aggregated under Hong Kong listing rules and classified as a discloseable transaction, triggering reporting and announcement requirements; the move underscores the company’s effort to enhance returns on idle cash while maintaining a conservative risk profile, with implications for shareholders in terms of liquidity management and interest income.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2331) stock is a Buy with a HK$23.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Li Ning Company stock, see the HK:2331 Stock Forecast page.

More about Li Ning Company

Li Ning Company Limited is a leading Chinese sportswear and athletic footwear company, designing, manufacturing and retailing sports apparel, footwear and related accessories. Listed in Hong Kong, it focuses on the domestic China market with multiple operating subsidiaries, including dedicated units for its core brand and kidswear segment.
